metadata.description: Diaphyseal femur fracture often managed with intramedullary fixation in adults.
Intramedullary nailing for unstable or operative femoral shaft fracture patterns.
Intramedullary nail fixation method for femoral diaphyseal fractures.
Two or more noncontiguous femoral shaft fracture segments indicating high-energy injury.
Angular, rotational, or length malalignment after femoral shaft fracture healing.
Systemic fat embolism syndrome risk after long-bone and femoral shaft trauma.

decisionPoints.trigger: Unstable femoral shaft fracture, segmental pattern, or polytrauma patient needing early stabilization
decisionPoints.action: Discuss femoral IM nailing with attending; document alignment goals and systemic complication monitoring

decisionPoints.action: Urgent irrigation and debridement with stabilization plan per attending and trauma protocol
